reddit_title: Got hit by a commercial truck in Austin — do I actually need a lawyer, or can I handle it myself?
reddit_body:
Truck accident claims in Texas are genuinely more complicated than standard car accident cases, and it's worth understanding why before deciding whether to go it alone. Commercial trucks are regulated by the FMCSA, which means there are specific rules around driver hours, maintenance logs, and load requirements—violations of any of these can establish liability, but you have to know to look for them.

One of the biggest issues is evidence preservation. Trucks have black boxes (ECMs) and electronic logging devices that record speed, braking, and hours of service. Trucking companies are not required to preserve this data indefinitely, and it can be overwritten or lost within days. An attorney can send a spoliation letter quickly to force preservation. There are also often multiple liable parties—the driver, the carrier, a shipper, or a maintenance company—which changes how a claim is built and negotiated.

If you're in Austin and trying to figure out your next step, a free consultation is worth doing before you talk to any insurance adjuster. Sites like wreckmatch.com let you get matched with attorneys who specifically handle commercial truck cases in Texas, at no cost to you. At minimum, get informed before making any decisions.
